e: Abundant ka matlab bohat zyada hai. Plentiful bhi same matlab hai. Isliye sahi jawab Plentiful.
Brief ka matlab chhota/mukhtasar. Short bhi same matlab hai.
Candid ka matlab khulay dil wala/sach bolne wala. Frank bhi wahi matlab hai.
Diligent ka matlab mehnati. Hardworking bhi same matlab hai.
Enormous ka matlab bohat bada. Huge bhi same matlab hai.
Ferocious ka matlab shadid/wehshi. Fierce same matlab hai.
Garrulous ka matlab ziyada bolne wala. Talkative bhi wahi matlab.
Hostile ka matlab dushman. Unfriendly bhi wahi matlab.
Imminent ka matlab anay wala/qareeb. Impending same matlab.
Jeopardy ka matlab khatra. Danger bhi wahi matlab.
Lethargic ka matlab sust/kamzor. Sluggish bhi same matlab.
Mundane ka matlab aam/pehchana hua. Ordinary same matlab.
Novice ka matlab naya/mubtadi. Beginner bhi wahi matlab.
Obstinate ka matlab ziddi. Stubborn same matlab hai.
Placid ka matlab pur-sukoon. Calm bhi wahi matlab.
Quintessential ka matlab sabse mashhoor/typical. Typical bhi same matlab.
Reckless ka matlab la-parwah. Careless same matlab.
Sagacious ka matlab Dana/danishmand. Wise bhi wahi matlab.
Tenacious ka matlab mazboot iraday wala. Persistent same matlab.
Vivacious ka matlab zinda-dil. Lively bhi wahi matlab.
Ancient ka matlab qadeem. Uska opposite Modern hai.
Barren ka matlab banjar. Uska opposite Fertile (zarkhaiz) hai.
Conceal ka matlab chhupana. Uska opposite Reveal (zaahir karna) hai.
Dense ka matlab ghana. Uska opposite Sparse (patla) hai.
Elevate ka matlab buland karna. Uska opposite Lower (neeche karna) hai.
Fragment ka matlab tukda. Uska opposite Whole (mukammal) hai.
Generous ka matlab sakhi. Uska opposite Stingy (kanjoos) hai.
Hasty ka matlab jaldi jaldi. Uska opposite Slow (dheere) hai.
Increase ka matlab barhana. Uska opposite Decrease (kam karna) hai.
Juvenile ka matlab nojawan. Uska opposite Mature (baalig) hai.
Ken ka matlab ilm. Uska opposite Ignorance (jahalat) hai.
Lenient ka matlab naram. Uska opposite Strict (sakht) hai.
Meager ka matlab thoda. Uska opposite Plentiful (bohat) hai.
Noble ka matlab shareef. Uska opposite Ignoble (kamina) hai.
Optimist ka matlab umeed-wala. Uska opposite Pessimist (maayoos) hai.
Persistent ka matlab jamay rahne wala. Uska opposite Yielding (dheemay) hai.
Quench ka matlab bujhana. Uska opposite Ignite (jalana) hai.
Ridiculous ka matlab behooda. Uska opposite Sensible (samarth) hai.
Shallow ka matlab thaos. Uska opposite Deep (gehra) hai.
Trivial ka matlab aham. Uska opposite Important (muhim) hai.
'Afraid' ke baad hamesha 'of' lagta hai. Afraid of = se darna.
'Good at' ek common phrase hai — kisi cheez mein maharat rakhna.
Specific jagah ke liye 'arrive at' lagta hai (station, airport, etc.).
Point in time ke liye 'since' lagta hai (Monday, 2010, etc.).

q: I have lived here ___ ten years.
e: Duration ke liye 'for' lagta hai (10 years, 5 months, etc.).
Surface par rakhi cheez ke liye 'on' lagta hai.
Bimari se maut ke liye 'die of' lagta hai.
Married ke baad 'to' lagta hai, 'with' nahi.
Junior/senior ke baad 'to' lagta hai, 'than' nahi.

q: The cat is hiding ___ the chair.
e: Seedhi neeche wali jagah ke liye 'under' lagta hai.
Point in time (morning) ke liye 'since' lagta hai.
Interested ke baad 'in' lagta hai.
Famous ke baad 'for' lagta hai — kis cheez mein mashhoor.
3 ya zyada logon ke beech 'among' lagta hai. 2 logon ke liye 'between'.
Prefer X to Y — hamesha 'to' lagta hai, 'than' nahi.

q: The meeting was presided ___ by the principal.
e: Preside over — meeting ko chair karna.
Congratulate ke baad 'on' lagta hai.
Absent ke baad 'from' lagta hai — kahan se ghayab.
Prevent from — kisi kaam se rokn a.
Charge with — kisi jurm ke ilzaam mein mukaddar karna.

o: He does not know to swim.
He does not know how to swim.
e: Sahi structure: 'know how to + verb'. 'Does not' ke baad base verb aata hai.

o: One of my friends are a doctor.
One of my friends is a doctor.
One of my friend is a doctor.
One of my friends were a doctor.
e: 'One of + plural noun' ke baad singular verb aata hai (is, was).

o: Neither of the boys were present.
Neither of the boys was present.
Neither of the boy was present.
Neither of the boys are present.
e: 'Neither of' singular verb leta hai (was).

o: He is more taller than me.
He is taller than I am.
e: Comparative (taller) ke saath 'more' nahi lagta. 'Than I am' grammatically sahi hai.
'News' uncountable singular hai — hamesha 'is' leta hai.

o: Scissors has been on the table.
A scissors is on the table.
e: Scissors hamesha plural hota hai — 'are' lagta hai.
Advice uncountable hai — 'some' lagta hai, 'an' ya 'advices' nahi.

o: He has gone to the home.
e: Home se pehle 'to' nahi lagta jab 'go/gone' use ho raha ho.

o: I have been living here since 2010.
e: Since ke saath Present Perfect Continuous (have been living) lagta hai.

o: Each of the boys have a book.
Each of the boys has a book.
Each of the boys are having a book.
Each of the boy has a book.
e: 'Each of' singular verb leta hai (has).

o: He is one of the best player.
He is one of the best players.
He is one of the best player's.
He is the best of player.
e: 'One of the + superlative + plural noun' (players).

o: I look forward to see you.
I look forward to seeing you.
I look forward to saw you.
e: 'Look forward to' ke baad gerund (ing form) aata hai.
Suggest ke baad 'that + clause' aata hai, 'me to' nahi.

o: He explained the rule to me.
He explained to me the rule.
e: Explain ke structure: explain + object + to + person.

o: The committee has agreed on the plan.
The committee have agreed on the plan.
The committee has agree on the plan.
The committee is agree on the plan.
e: Committee ek group hai, singular treat hota hai agar unit ke tor par act karta hai — 'has' lagta hai.
Habitual action ke liye Simple Present (eats) lagta hai.
Yesterday (past time marker) ke liye Simple Past (went).
'When I called' ke time ongoing action ke liye Past Continuous (were watching).

q: By next year, she ___ her degree.
e: Future perfect tab use hota hai jab future mein koi kaam mukammal ho chuka hoga.

q: I ___ never ___ such a beautiful scene.
e: Present Perfect (have seen) life-time experience ke liye use hota hai.
Nadyon ke naam ke pehle 'The' lagta hai (The Ganges, The Indus).

q: ___ Mount Everest is the highest peak.
e: Individual peaks (Mount X) ke pehle koi article nahi lagta.
'Honest' vowel sound se shuru hota hai (h silent) — isliye 'an' lagta hai.
'University' /yuː/ consonant sound se shuru hota hai — 'a' lagta hai.

q: ___ Quran is the holy book of Muslims.
e: Mazhabi kitabon ke pehle 'The' lagta hai (The Quran, The Bible).
Khelon (sports) ke pehle koi article nahi lagta.
Uncountable material nouns (gold, water, sand) ke pehle koi article nahi lagta.
'M' /em/ vowel sound se hota hai — 'an' lagta hai.
Unique objects (Sun, Moon, Earth) ke pehle 'The' lagta hai.
Umbrella vowel sound se shuru — 'an' lagta hai.
Double 'c' aur double 'm' — Accommodation. Yeh ek common misspelling hai.
R-H-Y-T-H-M. Koi vowel nahi pehle do letters mein.
Maintenance — 'e' ke saath, 'ain' nahi.
Double 'r' aur double 's' — Embarrass.
Privilege — 'lege' ke saath, 'ledge' nahi.
Double 'c', double 'r', -ence — Occurrence.
Ek 'c', double 's' — Necessary.
'i' ke saath, 'a' nahi — Definitely.
'a' ke saath, 'e' nahi — Separate.
Restaurant — 'au' ke saath.
